argument: The verifiable core of the contested claim is nuanced but partially supported by the available evidence. Devin Marsh did found DataLoom in March 2019 and claimed to have served as its full-time CEO [deck]. The timeline provided in the Corevance slide deck lists Marsh's role as CEO but does not specify exact dates, leaving room for interpretation regarding the overlap with his position at Stripe. While Marsh was indeed an Infrastructure Engineer at Stripe until mid-2020, it's plausible that he could have been managing both roles, as startup founders often engage in dual roles during initial phases [web]. This explanation does not confirm full-time dedication to DataLoom but accounts for the claim's existence. However, I concede the absence of verifiable documents for the $4M raise and acquisition by Cisco [web]. Thus, while some core elements of the claim hold, Marsh's full-time commitment remains unverified.
